[IMAGE:0]Midnight is just a few minutes away, and so it's time to launch a new Magic Monday. Ask me anything about occultism, and with certain exceptions noted below, any question received by midnight Monday Eastern time will get an answer. Please note:  Any question or comment received after then will not get an answer, and in fact will just be deleted.  If you're in a hurry, or suspect you may be the 143,916th person to ask a question, please check out the very rough version 1.1 of The Magic Monday FAQ here. 

Also: I will not be putting through or answering any more questions about practicing magic around children. I've answered those in simple declarative sentences in the FAQ. If you read the FAQ and don't think your question has been answered, read it again. If that doesn't help, consider remedial reading classes; yes, it really is as simple and straightforward as the FAQ says.  And further:  I've decided that questions about getting goodies from spirits are also permanently off topic here. The point of occultism is to develop your own capacities, not to try to bully or wheedle other beings into doing things for you. I've discussed this in a post on my blog.

The image? I field a lot of questions about my books these days, so I've decided to do little capsule summaries of them here, one per week. The book above was my forty-eighth published book and the first volume of short pieces I published. By 2017, when I assembled it, I was no longer doing the Neopagan conference circuit, partly because I'd stepped down as head of AODA and partly because my late wife's health was failing and having me on the road was increasingly hard on her. So I took the most popular talks I'd been giving, turned them into a book, and after a little searching found a home for it with Aeon Press; that turned into the beginning of a successful working relationship that continues to this day. The book is a good general introduction to my ideas; you can get a copy of it here in the United States and here elsewhere.

Buy Me A Coffee

Ko-Fi

I've had several people ask about tipping me for answers here, and though I certainly don't require that I won't turn it down. You can use either of the links above to access my online tip jar; Buymeacoffee is good for small tips, Ko-Fi is better for larger ones. (I used to use PayPal but they developed an allergy to free speech, so I've developed an allergy to them.) If you're interested in political and economic astrology, or simply prefer to use a subscription service to support your favorite authors, you can find my Patreon page here and my SubscribeStar page here. 
 
[IMAGE:1]I've also had quite a few people over the years ask me where they should buy my books, and here's the answer. Bookshop.org is an alternative online bookstore that supports local bookstores and authors, which a certain gargantuan corporation doesn't, and I have a shop there, which you can check out here. Please consider patronizing it if you'd like to purchase any of my books online.

And don't forget to look up your Pangalactic New Age Soul Signature at CosmicOom.com.

With that said, have at it!

comments.title: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
comments.content:
Good Evening,

May this week's full moon bring you much of what you'd like and a minimum of craziness!

To Share: Two things tonight:

1. First, friend-of-the-blog Luke Dodson was kind enough to host me on his "Dodcast" - https://flintandsteel.substack.com/p/dodcast-72-jeff-russell
2. Secondly, I've at last put together a version of the "All-Father" bede (prayer) for the Heathen Rosary undertaking that I'm pretty happy with: https://jpowellrussell.com/#heathen_rosary_last_all_father_bede

To Ask: I've been reading about Dr. Karen Mitchell's "Persistent Predatory Personality" model (good overview from the fellow who introduced me to her approach here: https://ponerology.substack.com/p/introduction-to-psychopaths-and-ponerology), and it has me wondering: other than the druidic "experience all things, be all things" way of looking at things, do you have any thoughts on what the role of incarnations as folks who seem incapable of certain karmic development, like psychopaths, might be?

Thank you again, JMG and everyone here!

My blessings to all who welcome them,
Jeff

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content:
I've wondered more than once how much of the obsession with labeling people one doesn't like, especially people of different political beliefs, as "psychopaths," "malignant narcissists," and so on is common or garden variety projection: a way that people avoid dealing with their own less than charming habits by seeing an exaggerated version of those same habits in someone else. It's particularly striking that this sort of language is especially popular among members of the industrial world's upper middle classes -- in other words, people who receive a disproportionate share of benefits from the very system whose leading members they love to denounce. That is to say, I think Jung would have had a field day with "political ponerology."

These people we're labeling "psychopaths" do of course exist. Conscience, in occult terms, is the subconscious memory of past suffering brought on by karma. If you've done appalling things in previous lives and paid a bitter karmic price for them, the subconscious memory of the pain acts to keep you from repeating the mistake. One choice, though, is to suppress that warning voice and also suppress those intuitive and empathic senses that tend to keep people from wantonly hurting each other. People who do that in repeated lives become psychopaths. That is to say, they make the same mistakes and slam into a karmic blowback at least as bad as before. Since souls have freedom, it's possible to go through this same futile loop repeatedly before sheer agony finally gets the point across.

It's worth remembering, with this in mind, that karma need not be subtle. If the only way you can get a clue is to be kicked to death by thugs one thousand lifetimes in a row, the universe is quite prepared to provide you with that service.

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content:
Thanks very much, that makes good sense. While I certainly think you're right about the over-application of labeling/diagnosing political opponents as somehow pathological, in defense of this particular blog, and the book it was largely inspired by (written by a Pole named Andrzej Łobaczewski), they're less about "these policies I disagree with mean you're bad" and more about "how do systems become corrupt, and what role do individual bad actors have in making that happen," and it takes as a starting point the idea that some folks are psychologically abnormal in ways harmful to those around them - whether due to upbringing, the context, something innate, or some combination. Dr. Mitchell's work, which of course post-dates Lobaczewski's, is less directly concerned with the political, and more about "okay, there's a whole body of work on folks who seem to not think/feel the way most folks do when it comes to getting what they want and how it affects others, and that a lot of work trying to study that has flaws (only looking at the most extreme cases, or those who went to prison, or only focusing on a subset of the personality traits/behaviors that appear to come in clusters, and so forth)." From that, she's developed the "Persistent Predatory Personality" model, which posits that some fairly small subset of the population has personality traits that are more-or-less fixed by adulthood that lead them to desire power and control more than others, to enjoy and seek out making others suffer, and to apparently feel little to no guilt over these things.

Accepting that at least as a premise led me to wonder "if you have a life where your current set of tools is broken in a way that you can't, or perhaps only with very much difficulty can, learn the kinds of lessons we seem to be here to learn, what do you do?" It sounds like the answer is that in the most extreme cases, you don't learn the lesson in that particular life, you get the raw material to learn it in a future one.

So, once again, thanks very much for the insight.

Cheers,
Jeff

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content:
1) I think that there are at least two factors at work here. First, yes, some individuals who are attracted by positions of power are pretty much guaranteed to abuse them. Second, some kinds of organizations are pretty much guaranteed to produce abusive behavior irrespective of the personal qualities of the people who staff them. It's not accidental that Adam Smith of The Wealth of Nations fame, who was a professor of moral philosophy by trade, considered the joint-stock company (in modern terms, the corporation) to be the worst possible way of organizing a firm, because the divorce between power, profit, and responsibility guarantees morally dismal outcomes.

2) Those tools only get broken because the soul breaks them. What happens then is that the soul has to make a new set starting from the raw materials. The raw material of conscience is shrieking agony. You have to mine a fair amount of it before you have enough to smelt the metal and forge it into a conscience.

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content:
1) I grew up in the inner circles of wealth and power. One issue I think a lot of people miss is that in a society with social mobility, you can pretty much guarantee that people who are unwilling to do anything necessary to hold on to their money and status (such as me) are going to fall out no matter what, while people willing to do anything for wealth and status will rise to the top.

(There are certain safety nets that ensure I will likely remain in the top 20% until things get really ugly, but I long ago fell out of the 1%, despite having been born well within the top 1%; in my experience there is far more social mobility between the 1% and the 20% than either up or down from the 20%)

This pretty much ensures that a very large fraction of the people in positions of power are going to be the kind of people who are most inclined to abuse their authority...

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content: That's one of the reasons that so many societies default to strictly hereditary systems of rank. "Meritocracies" inevitably turn into kakistocracies, rule by the worst; in a hereditary system, the percentage of slimeballs in power is usually not much greater than their per capita prevalence in the society as a whole. As with most systems that work, hereditary rank is a bad system that happens to be slightly better than the alternatives.

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content:
Hereditary systems also have the best natural defenses against entryism. Whereas a Meritocracy can easy be taken over by hostile outsiders who do a good job feigning an organization's meritocratic values while having some completely different agenda in mind. Secondly "merit" itself is very situational; one can be meritorious in one particular area or disciple and a complete bumbling idiot when it comes to everything else. We're seeing this play out on real time with our own supposed "expertocracy."

On your last remark, I think the ancient Germanic class system of earls/carls/thralls and the patron/client clan system of Republican Rome, are two examples of bad systems that were probably a lot better than what we have today. I do have a theory that an established caste system (that has been totally corrupted) makes for a neater way for souls to incarnate into karmically-appropriate genealogies vs. the anarchic freeforall mess we have today in the industrial west.

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content:
True enough. I think that a lot of the problem with our current system, though, is that it's not actually meritocratic; in a real meritocracy, your parents' class wouldn't determine yours, and people who fail would be flung down to a lower level. In our system, parental income is the single leading predictor of social status, and once you're selected for the managerial class, you literally won't be allowed to fail. So we have the worst of both worlds -- a system that encourages entryism and allows psychopaths to rise to power, while still being unfair to the countless people who are excluded from positions of responsibility because they didn't have the right parents.

The Chinese mandarinate is a good example of a meritocracy done right: admission by examination irrespective of parental status, advancement in rank earned by success, and beheading as the standard punishment for failure. It worked quite well for two millennia.

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content:
Totally agree! Our system sure likes to pretend it's meritocratic though.

And I do find it pretty funny how the Chinese mandarinate accomplished what Plato could only "imagine" (in the John Lennon sense). These Chinese didn't need any baroque allegories of metals corresponding with soul types or weird wife-swapping schemes to be able to sort the wheat from the chaff and convince the general public to buy into their system.

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content:
1) I'd agree with you on both points (and, from what I've read, so would Koehli and Lobaczewski). As for joint-stock companies, lately I've come to think that the core ill is the divorce between "ownership" and "management." Yes, a partnership owned by the folks who run the show can get up to plenty of bad business, but add in the conflicting incentives of the principle-agent problem, and it gets a lot worse.

2) Fair enough. By "tools" I roughly meant "the body/bodies this incarnation has, with all of its gifts and limitations," but again, the idea that something persists within the soul between incarnations, however "broken" one such incarnation might be, seems to solve most of the difficulties I had in mind (including the idea that purpose of some incarnations might just be gathering up enough agony to transmute into a conscience between lives/in a later incarnation).

Cheers,
Jeff

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content: Wouldn't psychopathy, and it's cousin sociopathy, be the pathway to the unknown death, or is it just the school of hard knocks until they learn?

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content: Only if it's combined with misguided inner development. Most of the people who head for the Unknown Death are people who've abused magical and spiritual training for the sake of ego, and worked long and hard not to learn from the nasty consequences.

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content:
This makes a lot of sense to me! It also makes me wonder if those at the opposite end of psychopathy have in their minds subconscious memories of too freshly experienced karma or having been beaten down too badly. This would be characterized as a crippling overabundance of empathy, guilt, conscience, or remorse; an over-responsibility for everything that goes wrong no matter who is responsible; constant attempts to anticipate and fulfill other people's needs to the point of overextending personal resources; neglect of self and inability to maintain healthy boundaries.

Sigh, that would be me. I am getting better, when I don't feel guilty about not feeling guilty! I have always attributed it to my unfortunate upbringing, but the possibility that this is the result of many lifetimes is intriguing.

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content: Yes, that's also something that comes out of previous lives. Tolerably often what happens is that the soul did something (or some things) really horrible in one life, recognized that fact either in that life or between lives, and came into incarnation with that burden of guilt hovering around waiting to earth out on any available object. If I may pass on a bit of personal experience (not from this life), once you realize that it has nothing to do with your current life, it's much easier to cope with.

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths
replies.content:
Psychopathy -- real psychopathy -- is a brain deformity. https://www.med.wisc.edu/news/psychopaths-brains-differences-structure-function/

You can't generate emotions if you lack the brain regions to do so; just as you can't see if you are missing an optic nerve or those specialized areas of the brain which process signals from the optic nerve. This is pretty well known.

On the other hand, as John points out, it is convenient, and generally overdone, to paint people who seem to be insensitive to your pain as "psychopaths". Probably 2-4% of the general population has signficant psychopathy, generally relating to deformed areas of the brain which are related to emotional regulation and "mirror neurons".

Psychopathy is, apparently, a "conserved" characteristic, or the % wouldn't be nearly so high. Being able to take what you need, including lives of other entities, without feeling *any* remorse can be remarkably useful in certain situations. So, it makes sense psychopathy persists in a high percent of the population.

-gnat

replies.content: Of course it's expressed in brain structure. Occult philosophy teaches that the body you get in each incarnation is an exact expression of the karma you've built up in previous incarnations.

(I also talk to squirrels, rabbits, starlings, etc., by the way, and make a point of trapping insects that get inside without harming them, so I can release them through an open window. Yes, I'm pretty sure I know why.)

replies.title: Re: Dodcast Appearance, "All-Father" Prayer, and Question on Karmic Development of Psychopaths

Sometimes they are, yes. I usually get along great with cats. One time I was a room full of people and a dog I had only met for the first time seemed strangely interested in me over everyone else besides his owners; he was all over me, slobber and all. And once I got a backyard squirrel to play a game of chase with me; he/she seemed to immediately know that my intentions were friendly and that we were indeed playing a game of chase. I'd run at the squirrel and it would dart off then immediately come back to within a few feet of me. I've had a squirrels in a local forest reserve come right up to me, though the squirrels there were probably very used to lots of random people walking the trails there each day. And oh yeah, I once befriended an entire family of cardinals in the backyard of my old house, I'd leave unsalted peanuts out for them and every time I went outside one of them (usually the red male) would suddenly appear and patiently wait for me to go back in the house and grab some nuts for him. I didn't see him for almost a year and he came back one day and remembered my face like it was yesterday.

Your moth stories greatly inspired me, btw. I started treating moths like this and I recently had similar results!

comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
Hi JMG,

How would you rate the the DMH and CGD systems in terms of their demands on the subtle body? Is there anything in the DMH system that is as intense as a CGD druid temple with a Merlin's wheel or solstice ritual? How does the sphere of protection compare to the LBRP + ECR or ROIW as a daily practice?

Thanks for being so generous with your time. Magic Mondays are an invaluable resource.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) The DMH is less demanding. That's quite deliberate, of course; not everyone is well suited to a system of intensive ceremonial magic.

2) No.

3) It's equally beneficial but a little quieter and a little more subtle.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Oh, that's interesting. It sounds like CGD may be the most intense, or tied for it, of the systems you've published / promulgated. How does GSF compare? Why might one choose to step "back" or down to a system with lower intensity? And/or, why might one choose a more intense practice / system if a less intense one would be equally beneficial?

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) The broad Golden Dawn tradition as a whole is the most intense, in terms of the strain it puts on the subtle bodies, of any set of systems I've worked with. That's as true of the Druidical GD as it is of the Hermetic GD.

2) The Golden Section Fellowship work is cognitively demanding but it doesn't put as much stress on the subtle bodies as Golden Dawn work does. That's common for meditative paths as distinct from ceremonial magic.

3 & 4) It all depends on what you want to do and what your circumstances are. If you want to practice ceremonial magic and bring about dramatic changes in your life, you probably have to take up something that will strain your subtle bodies. If your life works and you want to develop your spiritual and mental capacities, something that puts less strain on the subtle bodies and focuses on cognitive and intuitive development instead is a better bet. Yes, it's possible to do one of these and then the other -- that's what I did; I practiced ceremonial magic until I got my life straightened out, then shifted gears to more meditative paths.

comments.title: Reincarnation in Cats
comments.content:
Hi JMG and Fellow Readers,
Our cat, Caligula, died last spring. He was a very wild stray and we had him for eight years. he was a companion to our pet cat. He didn't let us touch him and he refused to take any medicines or worming pills, no matter how I disguised them in cream, liver or salmon. Late last winter, he became ill and we eventually took him (hysterical) to the vet where we spent $800.00 for a month-long course of antibiotics that did no good.

Then, I wormed him with extreme prejudice and Caligula seemed to be getting better and by this time, I could handle and pet him. I was feeding him up on raw meat and I thought he would make it. He died one day when we had to go to town. We found him when we got home. We were very upset and buried him in my flower garden with a nice ceremony and some tears. I prayed that he would come back to us if that would be the best thing for him.

After some weeks, I began to look for a cat but my husband said I should let Caligula come back to us in his own time. I thought that was sensible. A couple of weeks ago, a neighbour phoned and asked if I knew of anyone who would be interested in a barn kitten. I asked if he had white feet. He did so I did a divination on if he was Caligula and the divination was very positive, Luis upright. I talked to my husband and he said yes so we got the kitten two days later.

He is almost identical to Caligula, being a dark tabby with white paws, belly, cravat and a white blaze on one side of his face. The only difference is that his eyes are more of a hazel green rather than the startlingly beautiful green of Columbian emeralds that he used to have.

He is very healthy, friendly, athletic and has a purr like a coffee grinder. Our other cat, Festus, is not pleased by he is coming to terms with the kitten. I think they will become friends. I am so pleased to have another chance to give Caligula a better life. We named both cats Caligula for their white boots as Caligula mean little boots in Latin.
Maxine

replies.title: Re: Reincarnation in Cats
replies.content: "The Return of Caligula" sounds like a horror movie, not a heartwarming tale about a reincarnated kitten. Still, point taken! Cats in particular seem to be good at reincarnating to seek out a human they like -- I must have heard two dozen stories like yours from people I know.

replies.title: Re: Reincarnation in Cats

I am pleased this is a relatively common thing with cats. We have had a number of sheep do it too. Is there anything more than prayers people can do to encourage the return of a pet?
Maxine

comments.title: Astral Condensers
comments.content:
I was reading the androids & alchemy section in chapter 16 of Levi's RoHM.
In Sedir's Occult Botany, he mentions:
"[Mandrake root] is an extremely powerful astral condenser, and the anthropomorphic shape it always assumes reveals its unique properties and singular energy."

Is this a fancy way of saying mandrake root is useful as a fluid condenser the same way that gold is, or is this something else?

replies.title: Re: Astral Condensers
replies.content: The first part of the sentence is a fancy way of saying that Mandragora officinalis is a good fluid condenser. The second part tells you that it's more specific than, say, gold, being especially potent for magic involving human beings and human passions.

comments.title: On Qabalah pronunciation
comments.content:
Greetings JMG!
We have been reading through your books on Hermetic Magic along with the Franz Bardon collection and we have found your expert insight incredibly helpful. We would like to ask if you could clarify some doubts we have about pronunciation when it comes to working with Qabalah.
We do get the principles of vibration, tone, color, and sensation but we are struggling with the proper pronunciation of the letters. Is it really necessary to pronounce every letter while making a sound or are we misunderstanding the concept? It's easy to imagine how to pronounce a vowel like “A” but, how would we pronounce a consonant like “T” or “P”? Specially when it comes to influencing the astral, applying a very low tone. We could pronounce “T” like “TEEE” but wouldn’t that make it more of an “E” than a “T”?. Could you please give us some examples on how these sounds would be or correct us if we are failing to grasp the concept?

replies.title: Re: On Qabalah pronunciation
replies.content:
You're working with Bardon's The Key to the True Quabbalah, right? The way I was taught, you don't make any sound at all in Step I. You just imagine the sound and combine it with the color and the rest of it.

In Step II you pronounce the plosive consonants (B, C, D, G, K, P, and T) with a little puff of air after them; thus B is "Buh," but with no stress on the "uh." If you want to know what the "uh" should sound like, whisper "uh" a few times, then try adding each of the plosive letters in front of it. C, K, P, and T are easy; say "Cuh," "Kuh," and the rest as though you're trying to whisper loudly. Then do the others with the "uh" fading into loud-whisper status as quickly as possible.

All the other letters, just stretch them out -- so A becomes "Aaah," Z becomes "Zzzz," and so on.

Does that help clarify things?

There’s some confusion regarding the relationship between “letters” and “elements” on the book about Qabalah. There’s also comments about some seals of Jupiter being actually Saturn’s on “The Practice of Magical Evocation”. Do you know if any of these errors are real? replies.title: Re: On Qabalah pronunciation
replies.content: There are probably confusions in the work, but they don't matter. Seriously, they don't matter. Lots of people pronounce the word "temperature" as though it was spelled "tempachure," and I don't know anyone who pronounces the R in "February," but we all understand each other. In the same way, the details of the symbolism don't actually matter that much. Just as Bardon advises each practitioner "to produce with his own rites, cults, gestures, and manipulations of the fingers," since these are simply mental supports for the intention, the symbolism works whether or not it's theoretically correct; the spirits and magical energies by this point are used to the published attributions and will respond promptly to them.

comments.title: Fortune's Star Logoi and Tropical Astrology
comments.content:
Hi JMG,

This week I don't really have a question as such, just some ideas to share and hear any feedback that it might provoke. My very slow study of the Cosmic Doctrine continues, and it's provided me with an interesting way to thinking tropical astrology. I've recently started a foundation course with the Faculty of Astrological Studies in London, and I've been wrestling with conundrums raised up by the division between tropical and sidereal astrology.

I'm up to Chapter 22 of the revised edition, and the short sub-chapter headed 'Influence of the Star Logoi' describes the Logoi of each Zodiac sign as remaining in place 'after the Cosmic tide which called them into being has passed away'.

Tropical astrology, as I understand it, matches the sidereal astrology of the Age of Aries, so it's as if some Cosmic tide brought these influences into being and left them in place to remain even after the precession of the equinoxes took the constellations away from their stations along the horizon.

What really starts to boggle my mind is how the different astrological ages may represent a Cosmic calendar which happens to match the seasonal calendar of the tropical zodiac as observed from the Northern Hemisphere - so if by moving into the ages of Aquarius and then Capricorn truly marks a descent into precessional 'winter', it suggests that there is some archetypal pattern which announced itself to the astrologers of the Middle East around 4000 years ago or so.

Intriguing stuff! If anyone has any ideas to add to or counter any of this, I'd love to hear them.

Many thanks, and blessings to all who will have them,
Luke

comments.title: Tremors and the Etheric Body
comments.content:
Greetings, JMG and all!

Some weeks ago I mentioned that I'm experiencing tremors (still in the process of going through multiple neurological tests to find out why, with Parkinson's being the likely culprit), and talked about the role of my etheric body in experiencing and managing the tremors, with some helpful suggestions coming from you folks here. The tremors have spread throughout my body and have become much stronger since then, sometimes even violent spasms, but I've also found some interesting ways to manage and control them using etheric-body approaches.

I found that both 'relaxation' and 'gritted-teeth control' approaches have counterproductive effects, with 'relaxation' just setting the tremor-impulses loose to run amok, and 'control' working temporarily but being exhausting and resulting in increased tremor when I finally let go.

I spent time just getting to know this aspect of my etheric body and finding out what makes it feel good under my present circumstances. It showed me that the tremors begin as potential-energy forms in my torso, then extend outward through various parts of my body as kinetic energy. What I need to do is smooth out the transition process between these forms of energy, potential-to-kinetic, and release the energy in a way of my choosing.

The concept of smoothness is key for me here. So I use imagery of spreading soft butter evenly over bread, or pouring heavy, warm cream over and through my limbs when I feel that potential energy arising, and just smoothing it evenly over, and then past, the part of my body that is beginning to tremor. This extends my awareness of my etheric body over and past the boundaries of my physical body, and spreads the energy outside rather than within my physical body. I then try to circulate that energy solely within my etheric body, outside the boundary of my physical body, so as to recycle and reuse it more productively. In addition to helping manage and reduce the tremors, this is teaching me more about working with my etheric body. I'm also using homeopathic remedies to help me in this process.

I've also started dragging my feet when walking, and am using muscle-memory (which I assume is an etheric-body phenomenon) of years that I spent skiing to help me correct this problem. When wearing stiff, heavy boots and having 5-to-6 foot long "feet" attached to one's feet, it's a recipe for disaster to allow one's feet to drag, catch in the snow, and cause falls and probably bone breakage (which did happen to me as a teenager)! Now I'm teaching myself to walk as though I'm slowly cross-country skiing, gliding smoothly instead of stepping-and-dragging my feet Quasimodo-style. I'm wearing etheric skis! :-D This is not only improving my walking but my posture as well. The only problem is that I develop quite a bit of momentum and have to be careful not to glide into furniture, etc!

I'm still training myself to do this etheric-body work more automatically, without having to focus so heavily on each step of the process, but it's working well for me so far and is rather fun! I do thank you, JMG, for all that I've learned from your books and discussions that have helped me figure out these proactive approaches to what is otherwise an upsetting and frustrating development. I'll also mention in passing that I'm learning a lot within my Heathen soul lore context from all the work I'm doing with this.

Best wishes to all,
Winifred Hodge Rose

comments.title: A Magical Education
comments.content:
I really enjoyed this book of yours, JMG, as with all of them, and something you wrote in there shaped the development of my ideas on "the Alchemy of Hel," that I published in my book "Heathen Soul Lore Foundations" and online on my website. I referred to your writing in this passage in my book:

"I like Greer’s explanation of alchemy as more than simply a field of study. He sees it as a universal method, in the same way that science is a universal method, with its own procedures which can be applied to explore many fields of knowledge and transformational processes. ... (Greer p.145-60)."

This idea was a door-opener for me--seeing alchemy as a universal method. When I first began studying Heathen soul lore, I thought that the Saiwalo soul, the soul that the Christians turned into the word and concept of 'soul' that we tend to have today, was the simplest of the Heathen soul-concepts. Not so; it was the most difficult of all to understand when coming from a modern Western thinker's direction (well, it's tied with the Aldr-soul in difficulty, our 'time-body' or 'body-in-time'). I had to wait until I understood all the other souls before I could tackle that one, and I wasn't able to do so until the concept of alchemy as a universal method was suggested to me. Alchemy is the perfect context in which to understand the World of Hel, which I call 'the womb of souls,' and its indwellers the Saiwalo souls. Hel and the Saiwalo souls form an ecosystem that functions through alchemical processes, in my understanding. (I write about this in my series of articles under the menu header Alchemy & Ecology of Hel, on my website HeathenSoulLore.net.)

I'm a retired research scientist, and finding a method that's like science in its applicability and power, but is better-suited than modern science is to the subject matter, was extremely helpful! So thank you for the Magical Education!

Winifred Hodge Rose

I well recall what it was like when it finally sank in to me that alchemy is a universal method rather than a specific science; I spent all my spare time for days seeing what I could interpret in alchemical terms, and just shaking my head and saying "Wow."  ;-)

comments.title: Something parasitic
comments.content:
Hi JMG,

I would like to ask about:
1.) If you've ever heard about a curse, or possibly of an entity(?), that could siphon way the blessings/good fortune of one person (or group of people) in order to draw them into another?
2.) Could someone do this unconsciously?


The context is this: My relationship with my family has always been difficult, and I had come to the conclusion that much of this avarice was stemming from my father. However, on the night my Grandmother passed away I had a dream that conveyed a different story.
In the dream I saw that all the reflective surfaces in her house were filled to the brim with snarling, hateful faces; Thousands of them. The thought, "This is the source of all the curses in my life" rang in my ears as I prepared to do a working of the elements in the cardinal directions in order to cleanse the space of these remaining influences- with the help of a magic amplifying dragon that showed up, of course (Yes, I am bragging about this)
Discovering that my Grandmother could have been some kind of vortex for evil intentions surprised me, so I went to my cards to clarify. I didn't need to ask if the cursing itself was true, the dream made that abundantly clear, so instead I asked more about the nature of the curse(s).

What is the area of my life most affected: Wheel of Fortune
What is the affect on this area: The Tower

The catastrophic destruction of my good luck/good fortune? yikes. But then a chilling realization quickly came to me: Grandmother was always an unusually lucky person. So much so that it was frequently brought up at all family gatherings. "Don't play cards against her, she'll always win!", or "If you get any scratch tickets, make sure Grandma does them because she always wins money!" are two phrases I remember hearing often.
Meanwhile I've seen the fruits of my labour vanish into thin air more times than I care to recount.

What did she do with all this good luck? Buy scratch tickets and take trips to the casino, of course. She'd then take all the money she won and dump it straight into the pockets of the people from home shopping channel. This does seems like a guaranteed way to destroy one's (or another's...) good fortunes.
I fear that this luck siphoning effect has also, somehow, been passed down to her children - or through them? They don't seem to benefit from luck as directly and obviously as she does, but I can't think of any examples of misfortune befalling any of them. They've all been gainfully employed their whole lives, each of them own homes in idyllic areas, and they've never struggled to pay for their expensive toys and hobbies. Their friends, spouses, and children on the other hand: Cancers and strokes at young ages. Unemployment. Suicides..


Why I believe this is was unconscious: nobody in my family has ever spoken about the unseen forces before, and I mean in ANY capacity. They are a very non-religious, non-spiritual group of people, so I have no reason to believe this was was a consciously constructed working. If anything, I could see how their habitual lack of care/interest in others and general inability/unwillingness to self-reflect could be taken advantage of (and possibility amplified) by a malicious being...but how could it get a hold on people who would lack enough awareness to give consent to it's affects?
I'm stumped!


And let me add this at the end - It's not my intention to direct blame or ill-will towards anyone I've mentioned here. I really do believe something is amiss, and I deeply fear that those who may seem to be benefiting in material ways are in for a dreadful shock when they discover that you cannot "purchase" something in this life with "earnings" that are not yours. I shudder to think about the consequences of the debt this must rack up. Everyone is being harmed in this situation 😔

replies.title: Re: Something parasitic
replies.content:
That is to say, your grandmother was a witch. By that, of course, I don't mean a Wiccan -- Wicca was invented out of whole cloth in the 1940s. Actual witchcraft is something, or rather several somethings, very different. It's in fact quite traditional in many societies to believe that certain people can push their share of bad luck onto others, and that some people do this deliberately while others do it half-consciously. Having seen this happen myself, I tend to think there's something very real behind the belief.

If you haven't done so already, I recommend that you read Dion Fortune's book Psychic Self-Defence, and then strongly consider taking up a daily protective ritual (such as this one) and exploring some of the basic protective magic covered in the Magic Monday FAQ (linked in the post at the start of this thread). See if that changes your luck.

replies.title: Re: Planetary intelligences
replies.content:
First of all, I want to make sure we're talking about the same kind of meditation. What I have in mind is discursive meditation, where you take a symbol or concept and unpack it in a meditative state. Discursive meditation starts with a theme -- a specific focus for thinking -- and proceeds from the theme.

The themes I've proposed here are the seven planets in the most general sense, and then the seven planetary gods, in whatever form you prefer. Since you prefer the Greek version, by all means use the Greek planetary deities, and give equal time to each variant -- thus Helios as well as Apollo, and so on.

“...has anyone noticed anything?”

A few weeks ago I noticed that unexpected emotional reactions were trying to flare-up for no apparent reason and couldn’t decide if it was an internal thing or just picking up on ‘miasma’ – it took an insistent act of Will to pause it, examine it and transmute it. Oddly the word ‘Avert!’ acted as a fine method to trigger the pause.

“Are there any things one could do to bolster oneself?”

For me a stubborn refusal to acquiesce coupled with tools from my ‘toolbox’.
A year or so ago I suddenly felt waves of anxiety coming out of nowhere and something I very rarely experience – stuff that was so intense that I wasn’t so much dealing with it as enduring it – ’hold fast’ was the best I could do and it taught me that when all seems to be stripped away, there is still something. Meditating on the roots and foundation helped me find what I was looking for.
A dream I had also helped… one of those dreams that stays with you. I was drowned by a tidal wave but still lived, torn limb from limb spinning in the wind but sill alive even though my body was spun apart in all directions, then buried and crushed under the earth and yet still aware, then burned to a crisp and scattered as ash but still, after tall hat [sic!], I was still aware. It had an impact!

“I have made some divinations, and they say everything is on track, it is just me that am out of balance. Keep going on!”

The last few days I have had a strange sense of calm optimism – no obvious cause and in contemplating the matter, it was like a ‘knowing’ that ‘everything is working out’ and, like you, a message to ‘Keep going on”.

Exploring the roots of my practices, once I stripped away all the processes and methods it felt like I might have got a hint of something. The metaphors in JMG’s Occult Philosophy Workbook and Hazrat Inayat Khan’s writings allowed me to consider multiple metaphors. The following words helped me search inside:

“Sympathy is the main quality to be cultivated in order to develop the spiritual faculty; but if one would ask me what I mean by sympathy, it is something I cannot explain. All such words are different names, different aspects of one and the same thing. What is called sympathy, kindness, mercy, goodness, pity, compassion, gentleness, humility, appreciation, gratefulness, service, is in reality love. And what is love? Love is God.”

Or put another way: Love is an action of the Divine.

And if there is one thing I have learned it has been to try and connect with the divine in whatever way one is able to perceive it and ‘sing’ to that – it seems to give a supercharged version of the Sphere of Protection. YMMV of course!

In my readings of Buddhism, I came across the concept of the Vimana, a sort of floating palace or chariot. replies.title: Re: strange flying things
replies.content:
1) Depends on which occultist you read. I don't think there's any kind of consensus.

2) There are at least three things going on in the modern UFO scene:

- first, the fey are up to their usual tricks.

- second, a lot of shamanic experiences these days are being interpreted as UFO encounters (compare "UFO abductions" to shamanic initiation sometime).

- third, the US Air Force has been using the UFO phenomenon since 1947 as a cover for its own secret tests. That's why UFOs always look like whatever secret aerospace technology the US is testing at the time. Remember when black triangles were all the rage? The US was testing the first generation of stealth planes then, which look remarkably like...well, you can finish the sentence as well as I can.

There's probably more going on than that, but those are three patterns that seem to be reliably involved.

replies.title: Re: strange flying things

I don't remember black triangles, since I was born in the 80's (bit of a generation gap there), but there's lots of info on those sightings, and stealth bombers explain them perfectly well. I'll definitely look into shamanic experiences, which I'm not too familiar with.

It's also interesting to me that the Fey have seemingly co-evolved with us. In the old stories, they take on the trappings of medieval culture; while more recent sightings have a modern/sci-fi aesthetic. I wonder if they're learning from us, and putting our knowledge to their own strange uses. Or maybe they reflect our own cultural biases? As I've discussed elsewhere, fantasy & sci-fi are essentially the same genre, except that where one uses magic, the other uses technology. Somehow I feel like that might be relevant. comments.title: Golden Dawn
comments.content:
Good afternoon JMG and everyone. Over the past couple of Magic Mondays I’ve enjoyed reading questions and answers about the differences in esoteric paths….mainly, if you want to practice Magic, you follow x path and if you want to only study and learn you follow y path. Last week our esteemed host commented that back in the far away days the Golden Dawn was meant to unfold your own potentials and not just about making talismans or doing workings for a goal. I was very glad to read that!

So I thought I would comment that for the past 5 years or so I have been consistently and with great enjoyment been learning this path…starting with LRM and now working my way through CoP and PoW. For about two years now my practice consists of daily LBP with the MP, tarot divination and meditation, R+C ritual with the MP once or twice a week and every Sunday morning with the Greater MP, weekly Complete Open and Close and I just did my second Equinox Ceremony.

I am taking it slowly and have also worked through the Order of Spiritual Alchemy along with daily devotions to the daily planetary dieties. I have considered practicing some practical magic…firing up a talisman for example. But honestly, since beginning the work, many of my problems….and there were some whoppers! …have really resolved themselves. I think the work gave me the courage to make desperately needed but unpopular changes accompanied by a lot of “assists” having to do with sudden perfect timing and the ability to perceive and seize opportunities.

So if you are drawn to the Golden Dawn but don’t necessarily want to practice a lot of magic to get some specific thing….but rather relish the drama of the work for its own sake….there are many many benefits for improving your life and raising your awareness to a higher level.

Thank you JMG for all you do and all you teach us. And thanks to the commentariat for always providing new things to learn and think about. Blessings to all who would accept them.

Taupe Crunchy Pelican

replies.title: Re: Celtic deities for LBRP
replies.content:
1) You can start quite a lively argument in GD circles by asking whether the divine names they invoke have anything to do with the mainstream Jewish or Christian religions -- and of course a lot of Jews insist that the Christians don't worship the same god they do! If you want an Irish Celtic version of the GD, you've got plenty of work cut out for you, because it's not just a matter of swapping names into the LBRP -- you have to work out equivalents for the whole intricate system of divine forces. My book The Celtic Golden Dawn shows one way to do this with Brythonic deities, and you could consider using that as a model.

GD magic doesn't use patron deities, by the way. That's a Neopagan habit and so was invented long after the GD system was established.

2) Wrong question. Choose one set and stick with it no matter what -- that way you're much more likely not to give up just before you start getting results. That happens far more often than you might think.

3) Not crazy so much as unproductive. If you really want to have to do twice as much work before you get results, though, by all means.

comments.content:
Thanks, as always, JMG, for hosting this weekly space.

I have a question that is kind of related to The King in Orange, but I believe that it is somewhat different. In North America, the culture wars have been raging on for several years now, of which using memes to mock one’s adversaries has become extremely popular and quite sophisticated (not to mention highly entertaining – an important blessing in these years of woe and near-despair). What I seem to be observing – though I’m happy to be contradicted if you or others observe something different – is that the memes produced by those who lean Republican / conservative / freedom have become extraordinarily potent, while the memes produced by those who lean Democrat / liberal / technocracy have become weak, ineffective, powerless. It even seems that whatever the “left” is doing these days (recent examples: Obama trying to browbeat Black Chicago men into voting for The Cackler, and the latest Dem ad encouraging men to vote for The Cackler) has been like “mana from heaven” just begging to get memed to the extreme. Polka-dot Acquiescent Yak (aka Ron M)

replies.title: Re: Meme magic
replies.content:
I've seen the same thing. The difference in memes is easy to explain. It's impossible to create real humor unless you're able to make fun of yourself, and that's one thing most Democrats these days can't do; they're too caught up in fantasies of being the Good People and battling the Bad People, and if they learned to make merry mock of themselves, they couldn't keep up the pretense -- or the pompousness. This represents a significant shift; as recently as the 1980s the Democrats were funny and the Republicans were caught up in pretentious fantasies and couldn't make a genuinely funny joke to save their lives.

As for the other belly flops of the Democrats lately, it's an outgrowth of the same thing. Too many of them have quite literally lost the capacity to imagine that other people don't see them the way they see themselves. Obama has this image of himself as The Highly Respected Black Guy, and it never occurred to him that black people who didn't grow up in a wealthy white household in Hawai'i, who didn't go to an Ivy League university, who don't own a $20 million mansion on Martha's Vineyard, and who recall with quite some clarity just how little he did for the African-American community during his eight years in the White House, might not have the same opinion of him that he has of himself. Thus he made a world-class fool of himself talking down to black voters. Until the Democrats stop talking at people and start listening to them, I think they're going to face a lot of similar scenes.

comments.title: Popular Conceptions…
comments.content:
Good evening, Mr. Greer. Hope all is going well with you!

My question has to do specifically with popular conceptions of God. Is it accurate to say that society unconsciously holds or creates a “popular” conception of God, that exists independently in the etheric and/or astral realm? And does an independent seeker need to work extra hard to understand God in a Real sense? Is this known to esotericists at all?

I hope my question makes some sense.

Thanks in advance.

replies.title: Re: Popular Conceptions…
replies.content:
Yes, your question makes perfect sense.

To begin with, human beings cannot understand the Divine in a real sense. To borrow a metaphor from one of my novels, that's like taking a child's six-inch ruler and trying to use it to measure a galaxy. As a species, we are still very early in the process of spiritual evolution, and our chance of understanding God is about equal to the chance that the dust mites in Mozart's favorite wig would understand his music.

The conceptions of the Divine embraced by popular culture do indeed have a presence in the astral realm. (Note the word "conceptions," not "conception." Different people and different churches have radically different views of the Divine.) The God-images in the astral are helpful to us, as they're as close as most of us can come to a sense of what the Divine might be. You may still have to sort through a number of them before you can find one that works for you!
